pattern: Count pairs of non-decreasing + non-increasing arrays summing to nums using DP with prefix sums over valid split values.
hints: At each index, arr1[i] can range from arr1[i-1] to nums[i]; the number of valid continuations depends on the delta between consecutive nums elements.
Let dp[i][v] = number of ways to set arr1[i]=v. Transition: if nums[i] >= nums[i-1], dp[i][v] sums over arr1[i-1] <= v; use a prefix sum to do this in O(max_val) per step.
The total dp state is O(n * max_val) and each transition is O(1) with prefix sums, giving O(n * max_val) overall time.

edge_inputs.description: single element array: only (0, nums[0]) split
strictly decreasing pair forces arr1 fixed at 0
decreasing then increasing forces zero contribution intermediate

stress.description: random array length up to 200, values up to 50
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def countOfPairs(self, nums):
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        n = len(nums)
        if n == 1:
            return nums[0] + 1
        max_val = max(nums)
        # dp[v] = number of ways arr1[i] == v
        dp = [1] * (nums[0] + 1)  # arr1[0] in [0..nums[0]]
        for i in range(1, n):
            new_dp = [0] * (nums[i] + 1)
            for v in range(nums[i] + 1):
                # arr1[i] = v, arr2[i] = nums[i] - v
                # need arr1[i-1] <= v and arr2[i-1] >= arr2[i]
                # arr2[i-1] = nums[i-1] - arr1[i-1] >= nums[i] - v
                # arr1[i-1] <= nums[i-1] - nums[i] + v
                upper = min(v, nums[i-1] - nums[i] + v)
                if upper < 0:
                    continue
                upper = min(upper, nums[i-1])
                for u in range(upper + 1):
                    new_dp[v] = (new_dp[v] + dp[u]) % MOD
            dp = new_dp
        return sum(dp) % MOD
